statement: The Essential Eight (mandatory ML2 for federal agencies) targets patching, MFA, app control, backups and admin restriction — none of which agent-enterprise addresses; it therefore contributes nothing to the agency's mandatory Essential Eight compliance posture and shouldn't be justified on that basis.

evidence.quote: Essential Eight is mandatory for federal agencies to Maturity Level Two under the PSPF, with annual compliance reporting.

falsifier: This kill-claim over-reaches: agent-enterprise never claimed to be an Essential Eight control. The team rebuts by stating it targets ISM software-supply-chain controls (e.g. ISM-1730 SBOM) and DTA AI-assurance, not Essential Eight — a category error in the criticism, not a project defect.
